airingSchedule.description:
AiringSchedule is every not-yet-aired episode AniList has dated ahead
(usually the whole current cour), for the multi-week calendar view.

nextAiringEpisode.description:
NextAiring is the upcoming episode's release slot (RELEASING titles);
nil for finished titles or cache entries predating the field.

schedule.description:
Schedule is the flattened future release list (absolute episode numbers),
filled from AiringSchedule.Nodes here and from season episodes by TMDB.

recursive.description: required to delete a non-empty directory

flat.description: no subfolder: files straight into localPath

api.LocalStat.description:
Local: what the folder holds on disk. Only set for the local catalog,
where the question is "what do I have", not "what can I fetch".

name.description: new base name, no path separators

path.description: entry to rename, under an allowed root

airedMapping.description: resolve absolute episode numbers to aired-order S/E via TVDB/TMDB (endless series)

airings.description: every scheduled future release the provider knows (multi-week calendar)

behind.description: episodes aired per AniList but not yet available locally (the source release can lag the original broadcast)

fromEpisode.description: count only local episodes >= this (shared season folder); 0 = all

intervalMin.description: global setting, echoed for the UI

langWaiting.description: videos on the remote skipped by the dub/sub filter, target not yet local

lastQueued.description: files queued at the last check, -1 = none yet

lastResult.description: error text of the last check, "" on success

lastUploading.description: files still uploading remotely at the last check

mediaId.description: linked AniList/TMDB id → metadata (cover, episodes, airing); 0 = auto/none

mediaSource.description: metadata provider for a manual link: "anilist" | "tmdb:tv" | "tmdb:movie"

missing.description: gaps below the newest local episode (e.g. have 1,2,3,5 → 4 is missing), independent of airing state

nextEpisodeAbs.description: absolute AniList number, shown in parens when it differs

offset.description: {episode-N} renumber offset: absolute episode = local - offset (for showing the original number)

renameSeriesId.description: explicit provider series id for rename; 0 = auto

renameTitleLang.description: BCP-47 for the localized rename title; "" = Plex/system language

seenEpisodes.description: watched episodes from the linked AniList list

subfolder.description: write into local_path/<remote name> instead of local_path directly

wantDub.description: sync only files tagged with this dub language code (e.g. "Ger"); "" = any

wantSub.description: sync only files tagged with this sub language code; "" = any

stale.description: rows older than the scope's TTL

title.description: from the media cache, "" when unresolved

plexFolder.description: matching Plex folder basename, if any

kind.description:
Kind: heuristic movie/series classification, so films mixed into a series
library are told apart. "" when nothing is conclusive.

pending.description: Pending: metadata is being resolved in the background, poll again

locale.description:
ui language at registration time, so the verify email is localized
before the first locale sync can happen

label.description: display title, for the restore list

url.description: the plex.tv page the user opens to authorise

airedMapping.description:
AiredMapping: the library pairs AniList metadata with TVDB's aired
season mapping, so a watch created from this suggestion starts with it

folder.description: Plex storage folder of the show

tvdbId.description: series id for the rename profile

building.description: a background rebuild is in progress

ambiguous.description: no confident match - the user should pick

provider.description: ordering provider from Plex: tvdb | tmdb | ""

seriesId.description: resolved provider series id, 0 when none

seriesProvider.description: provider used to resolve the series id

seriesTitle.description: localized title of the resolved series

baseUrl.description: public origin of this instance, used in email links

envLocked.description: json field names whose value comes from an env var; the UI locks them

oidcProviderName.description: login button label ("Sign in with X")

plexSectionSources.description: csv of key:source (anilist|tmdb); missing key = by library title

plexSections.description: csv of section keys, empty = all show/movie sections

trustedNetworks.description: csv of CIDRs/IPs that bypass the login rate limit

keyValid.description: the key was accepted by TMDB

updateCheck.description: instance setting: is the check enabled

ordering.description: raw showOrdering value, for the UI hint

provider.description:
Provider is which catalog Plex itself uses for this library ("tvdb" |
"tmdb" | ""), derived from its episode ordering. The modern Plex agent
looks the same for every library and every show carries tvdb, tmdb and
imdb guids alike, so the ordering is the only thing that tells them
apart.

info.description: Self-hosted S/FTP anime and media sync: servers, catalog, downloads, watches, suggestions and auth. The interactive docs are served only by local dev builds.
info.title: WeebSync API

delete.description: Deletes every cache row of one scope (admin only).
Deletes exactly one cache row (admin only). The key must carry the scope's prefix.

parameters.description: full cache key (must match the scope prefix)
parameters.name: key

get.description: Pages through the cache rows of one scope, optionally filtered by key substring (admin only).

delete.description: Drops one server's remote index (admin only); the crawler rebuilds it from the root. Catalog matches are untouched.

put.description: Sets one server's crawler interval and per-crawl budget, 0 = default (admin only).

get.description: Reports the state of all background machinery: running jobs, match queue, cache stats, effective TTLs, Plex/AniList/index/watch info and per-server match stats (admin only).

post.description: Triggers one background job by name (admin only): plex-suggestions, anilist-suggestions, index-crawl or rematch. Crawl/rematch require serverId; rematch returns a queued count, the others a started status.

delete.description: Removes one catalog match row, manual or automatic (admin only). The folder re-appears as pending and is rematched automatically.

get.description: Pages through one server's catalog match rows, optionally filtered and searched (admin only).

put.description: Sets the cache TTL overrides in hours, 0..720 (0 = default), admin only.

get.description: Finish the AniList OAuth flow (state check, code exchange, token storage); redirects to /settings.

delete.description: Unlink the requesting user's AniList account.

get.description: Start the AniList OAuth authorization-code flow (redirects to AniList).
Report the linked AniList account and configuration state for the settings UI.
Fetch a single AniList media entry by id.

post.description: Set the watched-episode count of a title on the linked AniList list.

get.description: Search AniList media by title.
Watchlist titles (watching/planning) present on the user's servers, plus the AniList trending chart.

parameters.description: Set to 1 to force a watchlist refresh
parameters.name: force

post.description: Link an AniList account from a manually pasted pin-flow access token.

get.description: Reports whether OIDC is available, whether registration is open, the active auth mode and whether first-run setup is still pending. Consumed by the login page.
Reports the caller's chosen categories, which categories are available to them (admin categories only for admins) and whether SMTP delivery is configured.

put.description: Stores the caller's chosen categories, dropping any not valid for them (a non-admin cannot subscribe to admin categories).
Stores the caller's UI language (de or en) for server-delivered emails and push notifications.

post.description: Returns a session cookie on success, or a twoFactorRequired challenge (with a short-lived token) when TOTP or a security key is enrolled. Disabled while OIDC-only auth is active.
post.summary: Log in with email and password

post.description: Completes a password login by verifying the TOTP code (or a recovery code) against the short-lived pending token.
Destroys the current session and clears the session cookie.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's id, email and admin flag.

post.description: Probes a base URL (and common mount paths) for an OIDC discovery document and returns the issuer of the first hit. Open during first-run; requires an admin session afterwards.
post.summary: Probe a URL for an OIDC provider

get.description: Lists the per-IP auth rate-limiter state, whether each IP is currently blocked (admin only).

post.description: Unblocks one IP (by ip) or every tracked IP (all=true), admin only.
Creates an email/password account. The first account becomes admin. When SMTP is configured, later accounts must verify their email before login. Disabled while OIDC-only auth is active.
Stores an OIDC provider configuration during first-run setup, before any account exists. Only reachable while there are zero users; afterwards OIDC config requires an admin session.

delete.description: Turns off TOTP (and clears recovery codes) after a password re-check.

get.description: Reports whether the current user has TOTP enabled.

post.description: Verifies the first TOTP code, activates TOTP and returns 10 one-time recovery codes (shown once).
Generates a new TOTP secret (stored unconfirmed) and returns the otpauth URL, base32 secret and a data-URI QR image for the authenticator app.

get.description: Consumes an email-verification token and redirects to the login page. Public: the token embedded in the link is the secret.

post.description: Starts a WebAuthn assertion for the enrolled security key after a correct password, keyed by the short-lived pending token.
Completes the security-key assertion and establishes a session. The ceremony id and pending token travel in the X-WA-Session and X-WA-Token headers; the body is the assertion.

get.description: Lists the current user's registered passkeys and security keys.

delete.description: Removes one of the current user's registered passkeys or security keys.

post.description: Starts a discoverable WebAuthn login ceremony and returns the ceremony id and assertion options. Disabled while OIDC-only auth is active.
Completes a discoverable WebAuthn login ceremony and establishes a session. The ceremony id travels in the X-WA-Session header; the body is the assertion.

Starts a WebAuthn registration ceremony. Send X-WA-Type: key for a cross-platform second-factor security key; otherwise a resident passkey is requested. Returns the ceremony id and credential creation options.
Completes a WebAuthn registration ceremony and stores the new credential. The ceremony id and metadata travel in headers; the request body is the credential attestation.

delete.description: Deletes a file or directory under the download root. A non-empty directory needs recursive=true. Admin only.

get.description: Lists entries in a directory under the download root.

parameters.description: Path relative to the download root
parameters.name: path

post.description: Creates a directory (and parents) under the download root.

oneOf.description: Directory path relative to the download root

requestBody.description: Directory path relative to the download root

post.description: Renames a file or directory under the download root. Admin only.

get.description: Lists the authenticated user's downloads (most recent first, up to 500).

post.description: Queues a single file, or syncs a directory (all missing files), from a remote server.
Applies pause, resume, cancel, or delete to the caller's matching downloads (all, or only the given ids).
Cancels a batch of downloads by id; done, errored, or canceled entries are skipped silently.

put.description: Sets the global transfer rate limit in bytes/s (0 = unlimited). Admin only.

delete.description: Deletes a finished, errored, or canceled download owned by the caller.

post.description:
Pauses a queued or running download owned by the caller.
Resumes a paused download owned by the caller.
Cancels a download owned by the caller.

Pauses a queued or running download owned by the caller.
Resumes a paused download owned by the caller.
Cancels a download owned by the caller.

put.description: Sets the transfer rate limit in bytes/s (0 = unlimited) for a single download owned by the caller.

post.description:
Pauses a queued or running download owned by the caller.
Resumes a paused download owned by the caller.
Cancels a download owned by the caller.

get.description: Streams download progress for the authenticated user as Server-Sent Events.
Community reviews for a title from AniList or TMDB.
Report whether the configured Plex URL and token reach a server, including its name and the linked account.
List the Plex show and movie library sections for the settings checkboxes.
Missing-sequel suggestions derived from the user's Plex libraries, with remote folder candidates.

parameters.description: Set to 1 to force a rebuild
parameters.name: force

get.description: Returns the VAPID public key browsers use to subscribe to push notifications.

delete.description: Removes a browser push subscription for the authenticated user.

post.description: Registers a browser push subscription (endpoint + keys) for the authenticated user. The endpoint is validated against SSRF-prone targets before being stored.
Sends a test notification to all of the caller's push subscriptions, so a subscription can be verified without waiting for a real download.
Performs the given old→new renames inside one directory (jailed to the download root). Returns the per-file result; entries that failed carry an error.

requestBody.description: Directory path and the list of old→new renames to apply

post.description: Dry-runs the rename options against a plain list of names (no filesystem access); used to preview a watch's rename rule. At most 100 names are processed.

requestBody.description: Names to test plus rename options

post.summary: Preview renames for a name list

post.description: Lists files in a local directory and returns the dry-run old→new mapping for the given rename options. Nothing is renamed.

requestBody.description: Directory path (relative to the download root) and rename options

get.description: Lists the authenticated user's configured remote servers.

post.description: Creates a new remote server for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deletes a remote server owned by the authenticated user.

put.description: Updates a remote server owned by the authenticated user. An empty password keeps the stored credentials.

get.description: Lists entries in a directory on the given remote server. Defaults to the server root when no path is given.
List a server's remote folders enriched with AniList/TMDB metadata; unmatched folders are flagged pending.

parameters.description: Directory to list (defaults to the server root)
parameters.name: path

put.description: Set or clear a manual folder→media match (mediaId 0 = unmatch).

post.description: Re-queue automatic folder matches under a path with a cache-bypassing search.

oneOf.description: Path and whether to re-match all folders

requestBody.description: Path and whether to re-match all folders

get.description: Report the metadata scope marked on a catalog path.

put.description: Set or clear the metadata-source mark on a folder (empty kind clears it).

get.description: Distinct dub/sub language codes (e.g. Ger, Eng, Jap) extracted from the server's remote index; populates the per-watch language filter.
Report the provider, episode ordering, language and resolved series (or candidates when ambiguous) for the folder's show.
Full-text search across a server's remote index; multiple whitespace-separated words AND-match the file/folder name.

parameters.description: Search query; space-separated words AND-match the name
parameters.name: q

post.description: Dials the server and lists its root path to validate the stored configuration.
Drops the learned SSH host key so the next connect re-learns it via trust-on-first-use.

get.description: Returns the instance settings (admin only). Secrets are reported as set/unset flags only, never their values.

put.description: Updates the instance settings (admin only). Write-only secrets: an empty string keeps the stored value, "-" clears it. Env-locked keys are ignored. Returns the effective settings, including any OIDC reload error.

delete.description: Revokes the single machine API token (admin only).

post.description: Mints the single machine API token (e.g. for Home Assistant), admin only. Only the hash is stored; the raw token is returned exactly once.

get.description: Machine-readable snapshot of downloads, recent finishes, watches and disk usage for polling consumers (Home Assistant etc.). Reachable with an admin session cookie or a machine API token.

403.description: admin session required (machine token is exempt)

get.description: Finish TMDB linking (token check, session creation, account storage); redirects to /settings.

parameters.description: Approved request token (must match the cookie)
parameters.name: request_token

parameters.description: true when the user denied access
parameters.name: denied

delete.description: Unlink the requesting user's TMDB account.

get.description: Start the TMDB v3 request-token linking flow (redirects to the TMDB approval page).
Report the linked TMDB account and configuration state for the settings UI.
Resolve a single TMDB TV or movie entry by id.
Search TMDB TV or movies by title for the match dialog.
TMDB watchlist and trending titles present on the user's servers (watchlist needs a linked account).

parameters.description: Set to 1 to bypass the watchlist cache
parameters.name: force

get.description: Report whether the configured TheTVDB API key can log in.

parameters.description: Bypass the cached token and log in again
parameters.name: force

get.description: Resolve a single TheTVDB series entry by id.
Search TheTVDB series by title for the match dialog.
Better-quality copies (resolution / more sub or dub) of series already present.
Lists all user accounts (admin only).

post.description: Creates a password-login user account (admin only). Disabled in OIDC-only/-auto mode.

delete.description: Deletes a user account (admin only). You cannot delete yourself or the last admin.

put.description: Sets a user's admin flag (admin only). Blocked when roles are managed by OIDC, and the last admin cannot be demoted.

get.description: Returns build metadata and, when the update check is enabled, a cached best-effort comparison against the upstream repo.

post.description: Enables or disables the upstream update check (admin only).

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's watches, each enriched with linked media metadata, local episode counts, airing schedule, missing-episode gaps and completeness state.

post.description: Registers a new persistent watch on a remote folder, optionally linking media metadata, and kicks off a first sync immediately.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's watches.

put.description: Updates an existing watch's paths, rename rule, media link and language filters. Changing the remote or local path triggers an immediate re-check.

post.description: Queues an immediate check of one watch and resets its countdown. Reachable with a session cookie (own watches only) or a machine API token (any watch).
